@uncledullaz / day 45 — react state, lists & conditional rendering

> how to export:
  1. Wait 2–3s for Google Fonts to load, then click download all 11 slides.
  2. Top-corner logos use ../google-keep-logo.png and ../react-logo.svg.png (parent folder). Serve day-45/ over http://localhost so PNG export can inline them.
  3. Upload all 11 slides in order to Instagram as a carousel.

~/day45$ls components/

What I’m building.

APP
Root — owns note state useState([]) for notes · addNote uses prevState to append safely
FORM
Controlled form — child-to-parent calls props.addNote(note) on submit · conditional render: inactive ↔ active form
NOTES
Dynamic list renderer .map() over notes array · key prop on each · ternary: empty state vs list
NOTE
Stateless display card pure props — renders title & text · no state of its own in this version
[STATUS] notes fully wired up·form → state → list
03/11
~/day45$cat learnings.log

What I learnt.

01
useState with objects & prevStatestore object in state · always update via prevState to avoid stale closures
02
Child-to-parent communicationpass a function as prop · child calls it to send data back up the tree
03
Controlled vs uncontrolled componentscontrolled: React owns the value · uncontrolled: DOM owns it via ref
04
Stateful vs stateless componentsstateful holds useState · stateless only receives & renders props
05
Dynamic lists with .map() & key proprender arrays with .map() · key lets React track which item changed
06
Conditional rendering — 3 waysternary ? : · short-circuit && · early return if
[PROGRESS] day 45·zaio bootcamp | react
04/11
~/day45$cat App.jsx
[ CONCEPT ] USESTATE OBJECTS & PREVSTATE

Always use prevState.

// ❌ Risky — reads stale state snapshot const titleChange = (e) => setInput({ ...userInput, // may be outdated title: e.target.value }); // ✅ Correct — setter gives latest state const titleChange = (e) => setInput(prev => ({ ...prev, // guaranteed latest title: e.target.value })); // Adding to an array — same principle const addNote = (note) => setNotes(prev => [...prev, note]);
why: React batches updates — prev is always safe
05/11
~/day45$cat Form.jsx
[ CONCEPT ] CHILD-TO-PARENT COMMUNICATION

Data flows up.

// App.jsx — define function, pass as prop const addNote = (note) => setNotes(prev => [...prev, note]); <Form addNote={addNote} /> // Form.jsx — child calls it on submit const submitHandler = (e) => { e.preventDefault(); props.addNote({ title, text }); setTitle(""); setText(""); };
pattern: App owns state · Form just reports
06/11
~/day45$man controlled
[ CONCEPT ] CONTROLLED VS UNCONTROLLED

Who owns the value?

CONTROLLED

React state is the single source of truth. Every keystroke updates state; the input reflects state.

value={title} onChange={setTitle}
UNCONTROLLED

The DOM holds the value. React reads it only when needed, using a ref.

ref={inputRef} inputRef.current.value
// Form.jsx — controlled inputs (what we built) <input value={title} // state drives the UI onChange={handleTitle} // UI updates state />
prefer: controlled — predictable, testable, React-idiomatic
07/11
~/day45$man stateful
[ CONCEPT ] STATEFUL VS STATELESS

Does it remember?

STATEFUL

Owns state. Manages data. Decides what to pass down. App, Form, Note.

const [notes, setNotes] = useState([]);
STATELESS

No state. Just receives props and renders UI. Pure & predictable. Navbar, Sidebar.

const Navbar = (props) => ( <nav>...</nav> );
// In this project — who has state? App → notes state // stateful ✓ Form → title, text, isActive // stateful ✓ Navbar → no state // stateless ✓
rule: push state up — keep components stateless where possible
08/11
~/day45$cat Notes.jsx
[ CONCEPT ] DYNAMIC LISTS & THE KEY PROP

Render lists with .map()

// .map() turns an array into JSX elements notes.map((note, index) => ( <Note key={index} // unique identity for React title={note.title} text={note.text} /> )) // key tells React which item changed // without key — React re-renders everything // with key — React only updates what changed // best practice: use unique id, not index // index is ok for now — no deletes/reorders
key: must be unique & stable within the list
09/11
~/day45$cat Notes.jsx Form.jsx
[ CONCEPT ] CONDITIONAL RENDERING — 3 WAYS

Show it when it’s needed.

// 1. Ternary — two outcomes {notes.length === 0 ? <p>No notes yet.</p> : notes.map(n => <Note ...n />)} // 2. && — one outcome or nothing {notes.length === 0 && <p>No notes yet.</p>} // 3. if — early return (outside JSX) if (notes.length === 0) return <p>No notes yet.</p>; return notes.map(n => <Note ...n />);
pick: ternary for if/else · && for show/hide only
10/11
